pageDescription: Calculate the distance a car can jump based on speed, angle, and ramp characteristics for stunt planning and physics analysis

physics.title: Jump Physics
physics.description: Distance depends on speed, angle, height difference, and gravitational acceleration

formula.title: Distance Formula
formula.description: Range = (v² × sin(2θ)) ÷ g for level jumps, with adjustments for height differences

factors.title: Key Factors
factors.description: Speed, angle, weight, aerodynamics, and surface conditions all affect jump distance

criticalWarningsList.dangerous: This calculator is for educational purposes only
criticalWarningsList.safety: Attempting car jumps is extremely dangerous and should only be done by professionals
criticalWarningsList.legal: Many jurisdictions prohibit stunt driving without proper permits

importantNotesList: Optimal angle for maximum distance is 45° on level ground
Air resistance reduces actual distance for high-speed jumps
Weight affects acceleration but not trajectory in vacuum
Professional stunt performers use extensive safety equipment and planning

messages.enterInformation: Enter your jump parameters to calculate distance and flight characteristics
messages.resultMessage: Your jump calculation results are shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides theoretical jump distance calculations for educational purposes. Attempting car jumps is extremely dangerous and illegal in many jurisdictions. This tool should not be used for planning actual stunts.

seekHelp.title: Safety Resources
seekHelp.content: For professional stunt coordination and safety training, consult certified stunt coordinators and specialized training programs. Never attempt dangerous stunts without proper training, equipment, and legal permits.

errors.takeoffSpeed: Please enter a valid takeoff speed
errors.takeoffAngle: Please enter a valid takeoff angle (0-90 degrees)
errors.rampHeight: Please enter a valid ramp height
errors.landingHeight: Please enter a valid landing height

tooltips.takeoffSpeed: Speed at the end of the ramp
tooltips.takeoffAngle: Angle of the ramp relative to horizontal
tooltips.rampHeight: Height of the takeoff ramp above ground level
tooltips.landingHeight: Height of the landing area above ground level
tooltips.calculationMethod: Level of physics detail in the calculation

enter_vehicle_and_ramp_parameters_to_cal: Enter vehicle and ramp parameters to calculate jump distance
projectile_motion_with_initial_velocity_: Projectile motion with initial velocity v₀ at angle α
r_v_tf_where_tf_is_flight_time_to_landin: R = v₀ₓ × t_f, where t_f is flight time to landing height
car_jumps_follow_projectile_motion_princ:
Car jumps follow projectile motion principles. The vehicle becomes a projectile once it leaves the
                      ramp, with initial velocity components determined by speed and ramp angle.
reduces_range_and_increases_flight_time_: Reduces range and increases flight time due to quadratic drag force
vehicle_pitch_affects_landing_attitude_a: Vehicle pitch affects landing attitude and stability
this_is_an_educational_model_real_stunts:
This is an educational model - real stunts require professional
                        engineering
